title: Video Game Metadata Matching Pipeline Overhaul
description: Fix platform canonicalization, unify mapping files, wire in fuzzy name dedup, and add audit infrastructure

tasks.title: Unify platform canonical names into single registry
tasks.description: Replace the two separate JSON mapping files with a single utils/platform_registry.json that defines canonical platform names and their aliases. This eliminates silent platform fragmentation caused by inconsistent canonical names across files.

canonical_name_rules.playstation_spelling: Use 'PlayStation' (capital S) everywhere — Sony's official spelling. Fix all entries in both files that use 'Playstation'.
canonical_name_rules.ampersand_preservation: Use 'Nintendo Game & Watch' (official Nintendo branding). Update gamelist_folder_mappings.json entry from 'Nintendo Game and Watch' to 'Nintendo Game & Watch'.
canonical_name_rules.xbox_series_s: Add 'Xbox Series S' as a distinct canonical platform, separate from 'Xbox Series X'.
canonical_name_rules.single_source_of_truth: One JSON file. Each canonical name has an 'aliases' array listing all source variants (CSV names and gamelist folder names).

migration_steps: Build unified canonical target set using 'PlayStation' spelling and 'Game & Watch' ampersand
Cross-reference all aliases between the two files
Update all imports and function calls in merge_pipeline.py to use the new registry

tasks.title: Rewrite normalize_platform() for the new registry
tasks.description: Update normalize_platform() to load and use the single platform_registry.json. Fix the fuzzy fallback threshold and add logging for unmapped platforms.

changes: Replace _load_global_platform_map() and _load_global_gamelist_map() with a single _load_platform_registry() function
Build a flat alias->canonical lookup dict from the registry for O(1) exact matches
Lower fuzzy threshold from 85 to 75 for platform matching to avoid false merges (e.g., Xbox Series S -> Series X at 92)
Add logging (print or logging module) when a platform name falls through to unmapped — collect these for periodic audit
Keep the 'already canonical' shortcut (skip fuzzy if input is itself a canonical name)
Remove the consistency checker validate_platform_mapping_consistency() — no longer needed with a single file
details.new_unmapped_tracking: Add a module-level list _unmapped_platforms that collects platform names that couldn't be normalized. Provide a function get_unmapped_platforms() that returns the list for audit reporting.

tasks.title: Update all callers of platform mapping functions
tasks.description: Find and update all references to the old platform mapping files and functions throughout the codebase.

references_to_update: normalize_platform() — the main function being rewritten
get_platform_mappings_summary() — update to use new registry
normalize_source() — the call to validate_platform_mapping_consistency() inside the platform normalization block (line 689-691) should be removed
gamelist_parser.py — if it imports or references platform mapping functions directly
Any test files that reference the old mapping files

tasks.title: Add pre-merge within-source dedup
tasks.description: Before the main merge loop, run a lightweight dedup pass within each source to catch within-source duplicates. Use exact _name_match_key + platform matching (no fuzzy needed here since sources are internally consistent).

details.integration_point: In run_merge_pipeline(), after prepare_source() returns and before merge_into_main(), deduplicate each source DataFrame using groupby on (_name_match_key, platform) with pick_first for all columns.
details.logging: Print the number of duplicates removed per source.

tasks.title: Fix build_name_match_key to preserve meaningful punctuation
tasks.description: Current implementation strips ALL non-word characters, which breaks titles with meaningful punctuation like 'Game & Watch' -> 'Game Watch'. Preserve &, -, and apostrophes in the match key.

details.current_behavior: Line 350: re.sub(r"[^\w\s]+", " ", text) — strips everything non-alphanumeric
details.fix: Change the regex to preserve &, -, and ' (apostrophe): re.sub(r"[^\w\s&'-]+", " ", text). This keeps 'Game & Watch' intact while still stripping parentheses, brackets, periods, etc.
details.testing: Add test cases: 'Game & Watch', 'Super Mario Bros.', "Donkey Kong", 'Final Fantasy VII' — verify they produce correct match keys

tasks.title: Wire in fuzzy name dedup into run_merge_pipeline()
tasks.description: Integrate identify_potential_duplicates() into the merge pipeline with confidence-based auto-merge and a review queue for borderline cases.

details.integration_point: After all sources are merged (after the for loop in run_merge_pipeline(), before the collapse step), run fuzzy dedup on the merged DataFrame.

auto_merge_high.action: Auto-merge the two rows. Keep the row with better data (longer summary, more complete fields, higher rating if available). Log the merge.

auto_merge_standard.action: Auto-merge with same resolver logic as the main merge. Log the merge.

review_queue.action: Do NOT merge. Add to a review_queue list with both rows, confidence score, and the pair of names. This list is returned alongside the merged DataFrame or written to a log file.

implementation_approach: After the merge loop, if use_name_match_key is True, call identify_potential_duplicates() on the merged DataFrame

For auto_merge_high and auto_merge_standard: use the existing resolver logic to merge the two rows. The key is the _name_match_key of the pair (they should be similar enough to share a key after normalization).
For review_queue: collect (idx1, idx2, name1, name2, confidence) tuples
Drop the review_queue rows from the output (they stay as separate entries for manual review)
Return both the merged DataFrame and the review_queue (or write review_queue to a JSON/CSV file)
performance_considerations: identify_potential_duplicates() already has chunked processing and a max_comparisons limit — keep these
The dataset is ~482K rows; with the grouping heuristic it should be feasible
Consider running fuzzy dedup only on the post-merge DataFrame (smaller than individual sources since within-source duplicates were removed in P2-3)

tasks.title: Add audit/reporting step after merge
tasks.description: After the merge pipeline completes, generate an audit report with key metrics for validation.

details.review_queue_output: Write review_queue to output/review_queue.csv with columns: name1, name2, confidence, platform1, platform2, merged (boolean)

tasks.title: Add comprehensive tests for matching logic
tasks.description: Write tests covering the matching functions that are currently untested.

test_normalize_platform_exact_match.description: Platform name exists in registry, returns canonical name

test_normalize_platform_fuzzy_match.description: Platform name doesn't exist but fuzzy-matches to a canonical name

test_normalize_platform_no_match.description: Platform name doesn't match anything, returns original and logs

test_normalize_platform_ampersand.description: Game & Watch variants all normalize to same canonical

test_build_name_match_key_preserves_ampe.description: Meaningful punctuation is preserved in match key

test_build_name_match_key_strips_region_.description: Region/edition tags are stripped from match key

test_fuzzy_name_matching_known_positives.description: Same game, different names should score >= 0.9

examples: Different games -> not in any tier (< 0.80)

details.title: Update existing tests and run full suite
details.description: Update existing tests to account for the new platform registry and matching behavior. Run the full test suite.

changes: Update tests/test_final.py and tests/test_comprehensive.py to use the new platform registry
Remove any tests that reference platform_mappings.json or gamelist_folder_mappings.json
Add tests for the new audit/reporting output

key_constraints: PlayStation must be the canonical spelling (capital S) everywhere
Game & Watch must use the ampersand (official Nintendo branding)
Both 'Game & Watch' and 'Game and Watch' variants must normalize to the same canonical name
Fuzzy dedup auto-merge threshold is 0.90, with 0.95+ as high-confidence tier
Pairs scoring 0.80-0.89 go to review queue, not auto-merged
Xbox Series S and Xbox Series X must be distinct canonical platforms
Single source of truth: one platform_registry.json file
All existing functionality (resolvers, collapse_by_name, etc.) must continue to work

risk_assessment.P1-1_P1-2: Low risk. Data change + function rewrite. Platform normalization is well-isolated. Safe to test independently.
risk_assessment.P2-2: Low-medium risk. Changes the name match key, which affects all downstream matching. Must be tested with real data.
risk_assessment.P2-1: High risk. Changes the fundamental merge behavior. Fuzzy matching can produce false positives. The review queue mitigates this by not auto-merging borderline cases.
risk_assessment.P3-1_P3-2: Low risk. Additive changes (new tests, new output). No behavior changes.

rollback_plan.P1: Restore the two original JSON files from git. The old normalize_platform() code is still in the file until P1-2 is complete.
rollback_plan.P2: The fuzzy dedup code already exists and is not wired in. Reverting P2-1 means simply not calling identify_potential_duplicates() in run_merge_pipeline().
rollback_plan.P3: Additive changes only. No rollback needed beyond deleting new files.
